holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC just took an 8.94% single-day hit with no dated company catalyst on the board to explain or resolve it — that's a gap-down without an event to lean on. Until the next earnings date is confirmed or a headline crystallizes the move, there's no defined window to add or trim against; blindly averaging a broken tape into an information vacuum is the trap here. Wait for the print date to land or for price to base before treating this weakness as an add setup.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no dated INTC company catalyst on the calendar)
INTC.bull: A 9% one-day flush in a large-cap turnaround name often marks capitulation and sets up a mean-reversion window once an earnings date anchors sentiment.
INTC.bear: No dated catalyst to force a re-rate, and an 8.94% down day with a soft 5-day tape suggests the news flow driving this is still developing — could bleed further.

NVDA.read: No confirmed company catalysts on the board for NVDA and the tape is quiet — down 1.2% on the day, essentially flat over the week at $197.54. Without a dated earnings or product event to trade around, this is a hold-and-watch situation; the only thing on the horizon is triple witching in mid-September, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. Wait for the next earnings date to firm up before framing an add/trim window.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not NVDA-specific)
NVDA.bull: AI capex cycle intact and no near-term binary print means no forced catalyst risk — you own the compounder through the quiet stretch.
NVDA.bear: Crowded mega-cap; a broad de-risking into September opex/quad-witching flows could hit high-beta leaders like NVDA without any name-specific news.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down 9.4% over five days and another 2.7% today with no company-specific dated catalyst on the tape — this is a drawdown without a scheduled event to resolve it. Next earnings typically land in mid-September, which would overlap the Sep 18 triple witching, but nothing is confirmed here, so there's no defined window to add or trim into. Until a dated print emerges or the tape stabilizes, this is a watch — look for a confirmed earnings date to define the next add/trim window.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro; no ORCL-specific catalyst on calendar)
ORCL.bull: Quality cloud/AI franchise selling off with no bad news; a confirmed FQ1 print could reset the narrative higher.
ORCL.bear: Momentum is broken (-9.4% in 5d) with no dated catalyst to force a bid, so weakness can extend on flow alone.

NOK.read: No dated company catalyst is on the tape for NOK — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div — so there's no near-term event window to lean into on this 174-share position. The only thing on the horizon is triple witching on 2026-09-18, which is a market-structure event, not a NOK-specific driver. Until a Q3 print date or dividend declaration gets confirmed, this is a hold-and-watch, with any add/trim decisions better made on price levels than calendar.
NOK.bull: Networks spend cycle plus a clean balance sheet and buyback give NOK optionality if a catalyst does surface.
NOK.bear: Without a dated event, the name drifts with tape and FX; a soft macro print or EUR move can pressure it with no offsetting catalyst.

NBIS.read: NBIS just took a -17% single-day hit and is down -11.8% on the week, but there's no dated company catalyst on the board to anchor a re-rate — the only thing ahead is triple witching in September, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. Without a confirmed earnings date or a specific corporate catalyst, this is a knife-catch setup: you need to see either a stabilization pattern or a dated event (earnings confirmation, capacity/customer announcement) before leaning in. The single-share size means there's no urgency to act — let the tape tell you whether -17% was capitulation or the start of a trend.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, no company-specific catalyst on calendar)
NBIS.bull: A -17% flush on a high-beta AI infra name often marks a local low; a dated earnings or customer catalyst would give a reason to add on weakness.
NBIS.bear: No dated company catalyst means no forced re-rate — the drawdown can extend, and triple witching in September could amplify volatility in stretched high-beta names.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position is drifting quietly (+0.9% over 5d, +0.1% today) with no company-specific dated catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings or ex-div in the provided calendar. Without a print or dividend date to anchor a window, there's nothing to lean into here; the only marker is triple witching ~79 days out, which is a broad-tape event, not a KO catalyst. Wait for the next earnings date or ex-div to land before defining an add/trim window.
KO.bull: Defensive staple, steady dividend compounder; quiet drift higher is exactly what you want from this sleeve.
KO.bear: No dated KO catalyst means no edge either way — capital sits idle if a better setup elsewhere is dated and clear.

SPCX.read: Tiny 10-share position in a brand-new post-IPO name (SpaceX, June 2026) with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — coverage hasn't caught up to the listing. Without a dated company catalyst, the only real marker ahead is Sep 18 triple witching, which can amplify volatility in freshly-listed, thinly-covered names as lockup and options positioning evolve. Best posture is to wait for the first confirmed earnings date or lockup expiry to define a real window rather than trade the tape blind.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (only dated event on the board; first SpaceX earnings date not yet published)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity-value new listing with retail demand and no supply from a confirmed print — drift can continue until a dated catalyst forces price discovery.
SPCX.bear: Post-IPO names routinely gap on the first earnings release or lockup unlock, and with no confirmed date the risk is a surprise catalyst into a thin book.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
